steps.title: Identify what defines the Highwaymen's aesthetic
steps.content: The passage directly connects Demps's imitation of Hair's fast painting technique to the “impressionistic qualities that are now synonymous with the group's shared aesthetic.” It contrasts this with McLendon, who executed paintings with greater attention to detail instead of embracing the technique.

steps.title: Connect technique to group identity
steps.content: Demps's use of fast painting produces the impressionistic qualities that define the Florida Highwaymen's visual style, making his works more typical of the group than McLendon's. Choices A and C make aesthetic value judgments unsupported by the passage, while B misrepresents Demps as less derivative than Hair's influence suggests. That matches choice D — D is correct.
